EmDash – WordPress 的精神继承者，解决插件安全问题

EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security

blog.cloudflare.com 作者 elithrar 分数 520 评论 367 2026/04/02 00:14
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2026-04-0111 分钟阅读构建软件的成本已大幅降低。我们最近使用 AI 编码代理在一周内重建了 Next.js。但在过去的两个月里，我们的代理一直在致力于一个更加雄心勃勃的项目：从头开始重建 WordPress 开源项目。WordPress 为超过 40% 的互联网提供支持。这是一个巨大的成功，使任何人都可以成为发布者，并创建了一个 WordPress 开发人员的全球社区。但 WordPress 开源项目今年将迎来 24 岁生日。在那段时间里，网站托管发生了巨大的变化。当 WordPress 诞生时，AWS EC2 还不存在。在接下来的几年里，这项任务已经从租用虚拟专用服务器，转变为几乎免费地将 JavaScript 包上传到全球分布式网络。是时候升级互联网上最流行的 CMS 来利用这一变化了。我们给这个新 CMS 起的名字是 EmDash。我们认为它是 WordPress 的精神继承者。它完全用 TypeScript 编写。它是无服务器的，但您可以在自己的硬件或您选择的任何平台上运行它。插件被安全地沙箱化，并且可以通过 Dynamic Workers 在自己的隔离区中运行，从而解决了 WordPress 插件架构的基本安全问题。在底层，EmDash 由 Astro 提供支持，Astro 是内容驱动网站最快的 Web 框架。EmDash 完全开源，获得 MIT 许可，并可在 GitHub 上使用。虽然 EmDash 旨在与 WordPress 功能兼容，但没有使用 WordPress 代码来创建 EmDash。这使我们能够根据更宽松的 MIT 许可证来许可开源项目。我们希望这能让更多的开发人员能够适应、扩展和参与 EmDash 的开发。您可以将 EmDash v0.1.0 预览版部署到您自己的 Cloudflare 帐户，或者今天作为我们早期开发人员测试版的一部分部署到任何 Node.js 服务器：或者您可以在 EmDash Playground 中尝试管理界面：WordPress 的成就 WordPress 的故事是开源的胜利，它使发布的规模达到了前所未有的水平。很少有项目能够对互联网上成长的一代产生如此明显的影响。 WordPress 核心的贡献者及其数千名插件和主题开发人员构建了一个为数百万人实现出版民主化的平台；这个无处不在的软件改变了许多人的生活和生计。WordPress 永远有一席之地，但内容发布世界也有更大的发展空间。十年前，拿起键盘的人们普遍学会了使用 WordPress 发布博客。如今，人们同样有可能选择 Astro 或其他 TypeScript 框架来学习和构建。该生态系统需要一个能够为广泛受众提供支持的选项，就像 23 年前需要 WordPress 一样。 EmDash 致力于构建 WordPress 所创建的内容：任何人都可以以极低的成本安装和使用的开源发布堆栈，同时解决 WordPress 无法解决的核心问题。解决WordPress插件安全危机 WordPress的插件架构从根本上来说是不安全的。 WordPress 网站 96% 的安全问题源自插件。到 2025 年，WordPress 生态系统中发现的高严重性漏洞比前两年的总和还多。为什么在二十多年后，WordPress 插件的安全问题仍然如此严重？ WordPress 插件是一个 PHP 脚本，可以直接挂接到 WordPress 中以添加或修改功能。没有隔离：WordPress 插件可以直接访问 WordPress 站点的数据库和文件系统。当您安装 WordPress 插件时，您信任它可以访问几乎所有内容，并信任它能够完美处理每个恶意输入或边缘情况。EmDash 解决了这个问题。在 EmDash 中，每个插件都在自己的隔离沙箱中运行：动态工作线程。 EmDash 不是直接访问底层数据，而是根据插件在其清单中明确声明的内容，通过绑定为插件提供功能。这种安全模型有严格的保证：EmDash 插件只能执行其清单中明确声明的操作。在安装插件之前，您可以预先了解并信任您授予其执行的权限，类似于通过 OAuth 流程并授予第三方应用程序一组特定的范围权限。例如，在保存内容项后发送电子邮件的插件如下所示： import { DefinePlugin } from "emdash";
  2. 导出默认（）=>definePlugin（{id：“notify-on-publish”，版本：“1.0.0”，功能：[“read：content”，“email：send”]，hooks：{“content：afterSave”：async（event，ctx）=> {if（event.collection！==“帖子”|| event.content.status！==“已发布”）return;
  3. wait ctx.email!.send({ to: "[email protected]", subject: `New post published: ${event.content.title}`, text: `"${event.content.title}" is now live.`, });
  4. ctx.log.info(`Notified editors about ${event.content.id}`); }, }, });该插件明确请求两个功能：content:afterSave 来挂钩内容生命周期，以及 email:send 来访问 ctx.email 函数。除了使用这些功能之外，插件不可能做任何事情。它没有外部网络访问权限。如果它确实需要网络访问，它可以指定它需要与之通信的确切主机名，作为其定义的一部分，并且只被授予与特定主机名通信的能力。在所有情况下，因为插件的需求是预先静态声明的，所以总是可以清楚地知道插件在安装时请求的权限能够做什么。平台或管理员可以根据特定用户组请求的权限来定义允许或不允许安装哪些插件的规则，而不是定义已批准或安全插件的白名单。解决插件安全性意味着解决市场锁定 WordPress 插件安全性是一个真正的风险，以至于 WordPress.org 手动审查和批准其市场中的每个插件。截至撰写本文时，该审核队列已超过 800 个插件，并且至少需要两周时间才能完成。 WordPress 插件的漏洞范围非常广泛，以至于在实践中，各方都依赖市场声誉、评级和评论。由于 WordPress 插件在与 WordPress 本身相同的执行上下文中运行，并且与 WordPress 代码紧密地交织在一起，因此一些人认为它们必须继承 WordPress 的 GPL 许可证。这些现实结合起来，对构建插件的开发人员以及托管 WordPress 网站的平台产生了寒蝉效应。插件安全是这个问题的根源。当各方无法轻易相互信任时，市场企业可以提供信任。就 WordPress 市场而言，插件安全风险非常大，而且很可能您的许多客户只能通过市场合理地信任您的插件。但为了成为市场的一部分，您的代码必须以某种方式获得许可，迫使您将其免费提供给市场以外的任何地方。你被锁定了。EmDash 插件有两个重要的属性可以缓解这种市场锁定：插件可以拥有任何许可证：它们独立于 EmDash 运行并且不共享任何代码。这是插件作者的选择。插件代码在安全沙箱中独立运行：插件可以提供给 EmDash 站点并受信任，而 EmDash 站点不会看到代码。第一部分很简单 - 作为插件作者，您可以选择所需的许可证。与发布到 NPM、PyPi、Packagist 或任何其他注册表时的方式相同。这是一个面向所有人的开放生态系统，由社区而不是 EmDash 项目决定您对插件和主题使用什么许可证。第二部分是 EmDash 的插件架构摆脱中心化市场的束缚。开发人员需要依赖第三方市场来决定是否使用或信任它，而对插件的审查要少得多。考虑上面的示例插件，该插件在保存内容后发送电子邮件；该插件声明了三件事：它只在 content:afterSave 挂钩上运行它具有读取：内容功能它具有电子邮件：发送功能该插件可以包含数万行代码，但与可以访问所有内容并可以与公共互联网通信的 WordPress 插件不同，添加插件的人确切地知道他们授予它什么访问权限。明确定义的边界使您能够就安全风险做出明智的决策，并专注于与插件所提供的功能直接相关的更具体的风险。站点和平台越能信任安全模型提供的约束，站点和平台就越能信任插件，并摆脱对市场和声誉的集中控制。换句话说：如果您相信您所在城市的食品安全得到执行，您就会勇于冒险并尝试新的地方。如果你不能相信你的汤里可能有主食，那么你在尝试每一个新餐厅之前都会咨询谷歌，而且每个人都很难开设新餐厅。每个 EmDash 站点都内置了 x402 支持 — 对内容访问收费 网络的商业模式面临风险，特别是对于内容创建者和发布者而言。使内容广泛访问的旧方法，即允许所有客户端免费访问以换取流量，当没有人查看要发布广告的网站时，客户端是代表他们访问网络的代理，这种旧方法就会失效。创作者需要找到方法在这个新的代理世界中继续赚钱，并建立新型网站来满足人们代理的需求和愿意付费的需求。几十年前，新一波的创作者创建的网站成为了伟大的企业（通常使用 WordPress 为其提供动力），今天也存在类似的机会。x402 是一个开放、中立的互联网原生标准付款。它让互联网上的任何人都可以轻松收费，并且任何客户都可以按使用付费的方式按需付费。客户端（例如代理）发送 HTTP 请求并接收 HTTP 402 需要付款状态代码。作为响应，客户端按需付费访问，服务器可以让客户端访问所请求的内容。EmDash 内置了对 x402 的支持。这意味着拥有 EmDash 网站的任何人都可以收取访问其内容的费用，无需订阅，且工程工作量为零。您所需要做的就是配置哪些内容需要付款、设置收费金额并提供电子钱包地址。请求/响应流程最终看起来像这样：每个 EmDash 站点都有一个针对人工智能时代的内置业务模型。解决 WordPress 托管平台从零扩展的问题 WordPress 并不是无服务器：它需要配置和管理服务器，像传统 Web 应用程序一样对服务器进行扩展和缩减。为了最大限度地提高性能并能够处理流量峰值，不可避免地需要预先配置实例并运行一定量的空闲计算，或者以限制性能的方式共享资源。对于内容必须由服务器渲染且无法缓存的网站来说尤其如此。EmDash 不同：它是为在无服务器平台上运行而构建的，并充分利用 Cloudflare 开源运行时工作线程的 v8 隔离架构。在收到传入请求时，Workers 运行时会立即启动隔离来执行代码并提供响应。如果没有请求，它会缩小到零。而且仅按 CPU 时间（实际工作所花费的时间）计费。您可以在任何 Node.js 服务器上的任何地方运行 EmDash，但在 Cloudflare 上，您可以使用 Cloudflare for Platforms 运行数百万个 EmDash 实例，每个实例都可以根据您需要处理的情况立即完全扩展到零或高达任意数量的 RPS，使用与世界上最大的网站所依赖的完全相同的网络和运行时。除了成本优化和性能优势之外，我们在 Cloudflare 上押注于这种架构，部分原因是我们相信低成本和免费套餐，并且每个人都应该能够建立可扩展的网站。我们很高兴能够帮助平台将这种架构的优势扩展到他们自己的客户，无论大小。通过 Astro EmDash 实现的现代前端主题和架构由内容驱动网站的 Web 框架 Astro 提供支持。要创建 EmDash 主题，您需要创建一个 Astro 项目，其中包括：页面：用于呈现内容的 Astro 路由（主页、博客文章、档案等）布局：共享 HTML 结构组件：可重用的 UI 元素（导航、卡片、页脚）样式：CSS 或 Tailwind 配置种子文件：JSON 告诉 CMS 要创建哪些内容类型和字段这使得越来越多地选择 Astro 的前端开发人员和法学硕士都熟悉创建主题。已经接受过 Astro.WordPress 主题的培训，尽管非常灵活，但操作时会面临许多与插件相同的安全风险，而且您的主题越流行和常见，它就越成为目标。主题通过与functions.php集成来运行，这是一个包罗万象的执行环境，使您的主题既非常强大又具有潜在的危险。 EmDash 主题与动态插件一样，颠覆了这种期望。您的主题永远无法执行数据库操作。 AI 原生 CMS — MCP、CLI 和 EmDash 技能 使用任何 CMS 最不有趣的部分是进行内容的死记硬背迁移：查找和替换字符串、将自定义字段从一种格式迁移到另一种格式、重命名、重新排序和移动内容。这要么是无聊的重复性工作，要么需要一次性脚本和“一次性”插件和工具，而这些脚本和工具通常编写起来和使用起来都没有乐趣。EmDash 设计为由 AI 代理以编程方式进行管理。它提供您的代理所需的上下文和工具，包括：代理技能：每个 EmDash 实例都包含代理技能，向您的代理描述 EmDash 可以为插件提供的功能、可以触发插件的挂钩、如何构建插件的指南，甚至如何将旧版 WordPress 主题本地移植到 EmDash。当您为代理提供 EmDash 代码库时，EmDash 会提供代理按照您需要的方式自定义站点所需的一切。EmDash CLI：EmDash CLI 使您的代理能够以编程方式与本地或远程 EmDash 实例进行交互。您可以上传媒体、搜索内容、创建和管理架构，以及执行与管理 UI 中相同的一组操作。内置 MCP 服务器：每个 EmDash 实例都提供自己的远程模型上下文协议 (MCP) 服务器，允许您执行与管理 UI 中相同的一组操作。可插入身份验证，默认情况下使用密码 EmDash 默认情况下使用基于密码的身份验证，这意味着不会泄露密码，也不会出现暴力破解防御的向量。用户管理包括熟悉的开箱即用的基于角色的访问控制：管理员、编辑、作者和贡献者，每个角色的范围都严格限制在他们需要的操作范围内。身份验证是可插入的，因此您可以将 EmDash 设置为与 SSO 提供商合作，并根据 IdP 元数据自动配置访问权限。将您的 WordPress 网站导入到 EmDash 您可以通过转到 WordPress 管理员并导出 WXR 文件，或者通过在 WordPress 网站上安装 EmDash Exporter 插件来导入现有的 WordPress 网站，该插件会配置一个仅向您公开的安全端点，并由您控制的 WordPress 应用程序密码进行保护。迁移内容只需几分钟，并且会自动将任何附加媒体添加到 EmDash 的媒体库中。在 WordPress 上创建非帖子或页面的任何自定义内容类型都意味着安装高级自定义字段等重型插件，并将结果压缩到拥挤的​​ WordPress 帖子表中。 EmDash 的做法有所不同：您可以直接在管理面板中定义架构，这将为您创建全新的 EmDash 集合，并在数据库中单独订购。导入时，您可以使用相同的功能从 WordPress 获取任何自定义帖子类型，并从中创建 EmDash 内容类型。对于定制块，您可以使用 EmDash 块套件代理技能来指导您选择的代理并为 EmDash 构建它们。尝试一下 EmDash 是 v0.1.0 预览版，我们希望您能够尝试一下，提供反馈，并且欢迎对 EmDash GitHub 存储库做出贡献。如果您只是想玩一玩并想先了解一下 EmDash Playground 中的管理界面。要通过 CLI 在本地创建新的 EmDash 站点，请运行：npm create emdash@latest 或者您可以通过下面的 Cloudflare 仪表板执行相同操作：我们很高兴看到您构建的内容，如果您作为托管平台、插件或主题作者或其他活跃于 WordPress 社区，我们很乐意收到您的来信。请发送电子邮件至 [email protected]，告诉我们您希望从 EmDash 项目中看到什么。如果您想了解 EmDash 的主要开发动态，可以在此处留下您的电子邮件地址。Cloudflare 的连接云可以保护整个企业网络，帮助客户高效构建互联网规模的应用程序，加速任何网站或互联网应用程序，抵御 DDoS 攻击，阻止黑客入侵，并可以帮助您踏上零信任之路。访问 1.1.1.1从任何设备开始使用我们的免费应用程序，让您的互联网更快、更安全。要详细了解我们帮助建立更好的互联网的使命，请从这里开始。如果您正在寻找新的职业方向，请查看我们的空缺职位。开发人员Cloudflare Workers开源产品新闻

Original Summary

  1. 2026-04-0111 min readThe cost of building software has drastically decreased. We recently rebuilt Next.js in one week using AI coding agents. But for the past two months our agents have been working on an even more ambitious project: rebuilding the WordPress open source project from the ground up.WordPress powers over 40% of the Internet. It is a massive success that has enabled anyone to be a publisher, and created a global community of WordPress developers. But the WordPress open source project will be 24 years old this year. Hosting a website has changed dramatically during that time. When WordPress was born, AWS EC2 didn’t exist. In the intervening years, that task has gone from renting virtual private servers, to uploading a JavaScript bundle to a globally distributed network at virtually no cost. It’s time to upgrade the most popular CMS on the Internet to take advantage of this change.Our name for this new CMS is EmDash. We think of it as the spiritual successor to WordPress. It’s written entirely in TypeScript. It is serverless, but you can run it on your own hardware or any platform you choose. Plugins are securely sandboxed and can run in their own isolate, via Dynamic Workers, solving the fundamental security problem with the WordPress plugin architecture. And under the hood, EmDash is powered by Astro, the fastest web framework for content-driven websites.EmDash is fully open source, MIT licensed, and available on GitHub. While EmDash aims to be compatible with WordPress functionality, no WordPress code was used to create EmDash. That allows us to license the open source project under the more permissive MIT license. We hope that allows more developers to adapt, extend, and participate in EmDash’s development.You can deploy the EmDash v0.1.0 preview to your own Cloudflare account, or to any Node.js server today as part of our early developer beta:Or you can try out the admin interface here in the EmDash Playground: What WordPress has accomplished The story of WordPress is a triumph of open source that enabled publishing at a scale never before seen. Few projects have had the same recognisable impact on the generation raised on the Internet. The contributors to WordPress’s core, and its many thousands of plugin and theme developers have built a platform that democratised publishing for millions; many lives and livelihoods being transformed by this ubiquitous software.There will always be a place for WordPress, but there is also a lot more space for the world of content publishing to grow. A decade ago, people picking up a keyboard universally learned to publish their blogs with WordPress. Today it’s just as likely that person picks up Astro, or another TypeScript framework to learn and build with. The ecosystem needs an option that empowers a wide audience, in the same way it needed WordPress 23 years ago. EmDash is committed to building on what WordPress created: an open source publishing stack that anyone can install and use at little cost, while fixing the core problems that WordPress cannot solve. Solving the WordPress plugin security crisis WordPress’ plugin architecture is fundamentally insecure. 96% of security issues for WordPress sites originate in plugins. In 2025, more high severity vulnerabilities were found in the WordPress ecosystem than the previous two years combined.Why, after over two decades, is WordPress plugin security so problematic?A WordPress plugin is a PHP script that hooks directly into WordPress to add or modify functionality. There is no isolation: a WordPress plugin has direct access to the WordPress site’s database and filesystem. When you install a WordPress plugin, you are trusting it with access to nearly everything, and trusting it to handle every malicious input or edge case perfectly.EmDash solves this. In EmDash, each plugin runs in its own isolated sandbox: a Dynamic Worker. Rather than giving direct access to underlying data, EmDash provides the plugin with capabilities via bindings, based on what the plugin explicitly declares that it needs in its manifest. This security model has a strict guarantee: an EmDash plugin can only perform the actions explicitly declared in its manifest. You can know and trust upfront, before installing a plugin, exactly what you are granting it permission to do, similar to going through an OAuth flow and granting a 3rd party app a specific set of scoped permissions. For example, a plugin that sends an email after a content item gets saved looks like this: import { definePlugin } from "emdash";
  2. export default () => definePlugin({ id: "notify-on-publish", version: "1.0.0", capabilities: ["read:content", "email:send"], hooks: { "content:afterSave": async (event, ctx) => { if (event.collection !== "posts" || event.content.status !== "published") return;
  3. await ctx.email!.send({ to: "[email protected]", subject: `New post published: ${event.content.title}`, text: `"${event.content.title}" is now live.`, });
  4. ctx.log.info(`Notified editors about ${event.content.id}`); }, }, }); This plugin explicitly requests two capabilities: content:afterSave to hook into the content lifecycle, and email:send to access the ctx.email function. It is impossible for the plugin to do anything other than use these capabilities. It has no external network access. If it does need network access, it can specify the exact hostname it needs to talk to, as part of its definition, and be granted only the ability to communicate with a particular hostname.And in all cases, because the plugin’s needs are declared statically, upfront, it can always be clear exactly what the plugin is asking for permission to be able to do, at install time. A platform or administrator could define rules for what plugins are or aren’t allowed to be installed by certain groups of users, based on what permissions they request, rather than an allowlist of approved or safe plugins. Solving plugin security means solving marketplace lock-in WordPress plugin security is such a real risk that WordPress.org manually reviews and approves each plugin in its marketplace. At the time of writing, that review queue is over 800 plugins long, and takes at least two weeks to traverse. The vulnerability surface area of WordPress plugins is so wide that in practice, all parties rely on marketplace reputation, ratings and reviews. And because WordPress plugins run in the same execution context as WordPress itself and are so deeply intertwined with WordPress code, some argue they must carry forward WordPress’ GPL license.These realities combine to create a chilling effect on developers building plugins, and on platforms hosting WordPress sites.Plugin security is the root of this problem. Marketplace businesses provide trust when parties otherwise cannot easily trust each other. In the case of the WordPress marketplace, the plugin security risk is so large and probable that many of your customers can only reasonably trust your plugin via the marketplace. But in order to be part of the marketplace your code must be licensed in a way that forces you to give it away for free everywhere other than that marketplace. You are locked in.EmDash plugins have two important properties that mitigate this marketplace lock-in:Plugins can have any license: they run independently of EmDash and share no code. It’s the plugin author’s choice.Plugin code runs independently in a secure sandbox: a plugin can be provided to an EmDash site, and trusted, without the EmDash site ever seeing the code.The first part is straightforward — as the plugin author, you choose what license you want. The same way you can when publishing to NPM, PyPi, Packagist or any other registry. It’s an open ecosystem for all, and up to the community, not the EmDash project, what license you use for plugins and themes.The second part is where EmDash’s plugin architecture breaks free of the centralized marketplace.Developers need to rely on a third party marketplace having vetted the plugin far less to be able to make decisions about whether to use or trust it. Consider the example plugin above that sends emails after content is saved; the plugin declares three things:It only runs on the content:afterSave hookIt has the read:content capabilityIt has the email:send capabilityThe plugin can have tens of thousands of lines of code in it, but unlike a WordPress plugin that has access to everything and can talk to the public Internet, the person adding the plugin knows exactly what access they are granting to it. The clearly defined boundaries allow you to make informed decisions about security risks and to zoom in on more specific risks that relate directly to the capabilities the plugin is given.The more that both sites and platforms can trust the security model to provide constraints, the more that sites and platforms can trust plugins, and break free of centralized control of marketplaces and reputation. Put another way: if you trust that food safety is enforced in your city, you’ll be adventurous and try new places. If you can’t trust that there might be a staple in your soup, you’ll be consulting Google before every new place you try, and it’s harder for everyone to open new restaurants. Every EmDash site has x402 support built in — charge for access to content The business model of the web is at risk, particularly for content creators and publishers. The old way of making content widely accessible, allowing all clients free access in exchange for traffic, breaks when there is no human looking at a site to advertise to, and the client is instead their agent accessing the web on their behalf. Creators need ways to continue to make money in this new world of agents, and to build new kinds of websites that serve what people’s agents need and will pay for. Decades ago a new wave of creators created websites that became great businesses (often using WordPress to power them) and a similar opportunity exists today.x402 is an open, neutral standard for Internet-native payments. It lets anyone on the Internet easily charge, and any client pay on-demand, on a pay-per-use basis. A client, such as an agent, sends a HTTP request and receives a HTTP 402 Payment Required status code. In response, the client pays for access on-demand, and the server can let the client through to the requested content.EmDash has built-in support for x402. This means anyone with an EmDash site can charge for access to their content without requiring subscriptions and with zero engineering work. All you need to do is configure which content should require payment, set how much to charge, and provide a Wallet address. The request/response flow ends up looking like this: Every EmDash site has a built-in business model for the AI era. Solving scale-to-zero for WordPress hosting platforms WordPress is not serverless: it requires provisioning and managing servers, scaling them up and down like a traditional web application. To maximize performance, and to be able to handle traffic spikes, there’s no avoiding the need to pre-provision instances and run some amount of idle compute, or share resources in ways that limit performance. This is particularly true for sites with content that must be server rendered and cannot be cached.EmDash is different: it’s built to run on serverless platforms, and make the most out of the v8 isolate architecture of Cloudflare’s open source runtime workerd. On an incoming request, the Workers runtime instantly spins up an isolate to execute code and serve a response. It scales back down to zero if there are no requests. And it only bills for CPU time (time spent doing actual work). You can run EmDash anywhere, on any Node.js server — but on Cloudflare you can run millions of instances of EmDash using Cloudflare for Platforms that each instantly scale fully to zero or up to as many RPS as you need to handle, using the exact same network and runtime that the biggest websites in the world rely on.Beyond cost optimizations and performance benefits, we’ve bet on this architecture at Cloudflare in part because we believe in having low cost and free tiers, and that everyone should be able to build websites that scale. We’re excited to help platforms extend the benefits of this architecture to their own customers, both big and small. Modern frontend theming and architecture via Astro EmDash is powered by Astro, the web framework for content-driven websites. To create an EmDash theme, you create an Astro project that includes:Pages: Astro routes for rendering content (homepage, blog posts, archives, etc.)Layouts: Shared HTML structureComponents: Reusable UI elements (navigation, cards, footers)Styles: CSS or Tailwind configurationA seed file: JSON that tells the CMS what content types and fields to createThis makes creating themes familiar to frontend developers who are increasingly choosing Astro, and to LLMs which are already trained on Astro.WordPress themes, though incredibly flexible, operate with a lot of the same security risks as plugins, and the more popular and commonplace your theme, the more of a target it is. Themes run through integrating with functions.php which is an all-encompassing execution environment, enabling your theme to be both incredibly powerful and potentially dangerous. EmDash themes, as with dynamic plugins, turns this expectation on its head. Your theme can never perform database operations. An AI Native CMS — MCP, CLI, and Skills for EmDash The least fun part about working with any CMS is doing the rote migration of content: finding and replacing strings, migrating custom fields from one format to another, renaming, reordering and moving things around. This is either boring repetitive work or requires one-off scripts and “single-use” plugins and tools that are usually neither fun to write nor to use.EmDash is designed to be managed programmatically by your AI agents. It provides the context and the tools that your agents need, including:Agent Skills: Each EmDash instance includes Agent Skills that describe to your agent the capabilities EmDash can provide to plugins, the hooks that can trigger plugins, guidance on how to structure a plugin, and even how to port legacy WordPress themes to EmDash natively. When you give an agent an EmDash codebase, EmDash provides everything the agent needs to be able to customize your site in the way you need.EmDash CLI: The EmDash CLI enables your agent to interact programmatically with your local or remote instance of EmDash. You can upload media, search for content, create and manage schemas, and do the same set of things you can do in the Admin UI.Built-in MCP Server: Every EmDash instance provides its own remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, allowing you to do the same set of things you can do in the Admin UI. Pluggable authentication, with Passkeys by default EmDash uses passkey-based authentication by default, meaning there are no passwords to leak and no brute-force vectors to defend against. User management includes familiar role-based access control out of the box: administrators, editors, authors, and contributors, each scoped strictly to the actions they need. Authentication is pluggable, so you can set EmDash up to work with your SSO provider, and automatically provision access based on IdP metadata. Import your WordPress sites to EmDash You can import an existing WordPress site by either going to WordPress admin and exporting a WXR file, or by installing the EmDash Exporter plugin on a WordPress site, which configures a secure endpoint that is only exposed to you, and protected by a WordPress Application Password you control. Migrating content takes just a few minutes, and automatically works to bring any attached media into EmDash’s media library. Creating any custom content types on WordPress that are not a Post or a Page has meant installing heavy plugins like Advanced Custom Fields, and squeezing the result into a crowded WordPress posts table. EmDash does things differently: you can define a schema directly in the admin panel, which will create entirely new EmDash collections for you, separately ordered in the database. On import, you can use the same capabilities to take any custom post types from WordPress, and create an EmDash content type from it. For bespoke blocks, you can use the EmDash Block Kit Agent Skill to instruct your agent of choice and build them for EmDash. Try it EmDash is v0.1.0 preview, and we’d love you to try it, give feedback, and we welcome contributions to the EmDash GitHub repository.If you’re just playing around and want to first understand what’s possible — try out the admin interface in the EmDash Playground.To create a new EmDash site locally, via the CLI, run:npm create emdash@latestOr you can do the same via the Cloudflare dashboard below:We’re excited to see what you build, and if you're active in the WordPress community, as a hosting platform, a plugin or theme author, or otherwise — we’d love to hear from you. Email us at [email protected], and tell us what you’d like to see from the EmDash project.If you want to stay up to date with major EmDash developments, you can leave your email address here.Cloudflare's connectivity cloud protects entire corporate networks, helps customers build Internet-scale applications efficiently, accelerates any website or Internet application, wards off DDoS attacks, keeps hackers at bay, and can help you on your journey to Zero Trust.Visit 1.1.1.1 from any device to get started with our free app that makes your Internet faster and safer.To learn more about our mission to help build a better Internet, start here. If you're looking for a new career direction, check out our open positions. DevelopersCloudflare WorkersOpen SourceProduct News

评论（默认中文）

embedding-shape 2026/04/02 00:32
> 我们对这个新 CMS 的名称是 EmDash。我们认为它是 WordPress 的精神继承者。它完全用 TypeScript 编写。它是无服务器的，但您可以在自己的硬件或您选择的任何平台上运行它。插件被安全地沙箱化，并且可以通过 Dynamic Workers 在自己的隔离区中运行，从而解决了 WordPress 插件架构的基本安全问题。在底层，EmDash 由 Astro 提供支持，Astro 是内容驱动型网站最快的 Web 框架。

对我来说，这听起来与 CMS 需要走的方向截然相反，而是简化并回到“网站”根源，其中网站在任何地方都是静态文件，它速度快、易于缓存，并且比服务器端渲染的网站更容易处理。

但当然，这样他们就无法销售自己的“工人”产品，所以突然我想我可能会理解为什么他们会以他们的方式构建它，至少是为了测试他们自己的东西。

我不确定它实际上是否解决了“基本安全问题”，但我想这还有待观察。

> Our name for this new CMS is EmDash. We think of it as the spiritual successor to WordPress. It’s written entirely in TypeScript. It is serverless, but you can run it on your own hardware or any platform you choose. Plugins are securely sandboxed and can run in their own isolate, via Dynamic Workers, solving the fundamental security problem with the WordPress plugin architecture. And under the hood, EmDash is powered by Astro, the fastest web framework for content-driven websites.

To me this sounds of the polar opposite of the direction CMS's need to go, instead simplify and go back to the "websites" roots where a website are static files wherever, it's fast, easy to cache and just so much easier to deal with than server-side rendered websites.

But of course, then they wouldn't be able to sell their own "workers" product, so suddenly I think I might understand why they built it the way they built it, at the very least to dogfood their own stuff.

I'm not sure it actually solves the "fundamental security problem" in actuality though, but I guess that remains to be seen.

在 HN 中查看
perlgeek 2026/04/02 04:22
我喜欢构建静态（或静态生成）网站，但客户常常需要动态内容。更糟糕的是，他们不会预先告诉你，因为他们并不真正理解其中的区别。

“我的面包店需要一个网站”。 “上面应该写什么？” “我们的地址、开放时间、几张照片”。我为他们建立了一个静态网站。

“现在我需要一份联系表”。好吧，这并不适合静态网站，但我可以将一些东西组合在一起。 “现在我需要显示库存，并允许客户预购”。静态网站不再有效。

当您为客户开发时，尤其是那些您不太了解的客户，将自己逼入一个不太可扩展的角落是一个坏主意。所以从这个角度来看，我真的明白为什么他们给插件如此重要的位置。

I love building static (or statically generated) websites, but all too often, customers want dynamic content. And what's worse, they don't tell you up-front, because they don't really understand the difference.

"I need a website for my bakery". "What's supposed to be on it?" "Our address, opening times, a few pictures". I build them a static website.

"Now I need a contact form". Ok, that doesn't really fit into a static website, but I can hack something together. "Now I need to show inventory, and allow customers to pre-order". A static website won't cut it anymore.

When you develop for clients, especially those that you don't know very well, it's a bad idea to back yourself into a corner that's not very extensible. So from that perspective, I really get why they give plugins such a central spot.

在 HN 中查看
yurishimo 2026/04/02 04:46
这就是 WordPress 至今仍然如此受欢迎的主要原因。您可以将前端的垃圾缓存到那时它基本上是一个静态站点，但如果您将来需要这种灵活性，那么它仍然在动态平台上运行。

我像许多自学开发者一样从 webdev 开始使用 WordPress 网站，现在我已经转向更注重“工程”的开发范例，我肯定看到了痛点，但 WP 的价值主张始终是清晰且存在的。

鉴于目前 WP 的领导地位，我可以看到 Cloudflare 如何将其视为一个进入并剥夺一些市场份额的机会，因为他们可以说服这些当前的 WP 开发人员采用一点人工智能帮助并为他们的平台编写应用程序。

让我们看看是否有回报！

This is the main reason why WordPress is so popular still to this day. You can cache the crap out of the frontend to the point that it’s basically a static site at that point but then it’s still all running on top of a dynamic platform if you need that flexibility in the future.

I got my start in webdev slinging WordPress sites like a lot of self taught devs and I definitely see the pain points now that I’ve moved on to more “engineering” focused development paradigms but the value proposition of WP has always been clear and present.

Given how WP leadership is all over the place at the moment, I can see how Cloudflare sees this as an opportunity to come in and peel away some market share when they can convince these current WP devs to adopt a little AI help and write applications for their platform instead.

Let’s see if it pays off!

在 HN 中查看
BodyCulture 2026/04/02 13:21
以静态 html 或任何其他格式分发内容是管理内容的一个非常小的方面，并且对于当今的任何 CMS 来说，这基本上是一个已解决的问题。专注于这个最小的方面似乎很奇怪，因为要让潜在的大量内容能够由具有不同技能、责任和关系的潜在非常异构的内容创建者群体实际管理，存在更大的挑战。
Distribution of the content as static html or in any other format is a very tiny aspect of managing content and mostly a solved problem for any CMS nowadays. Focusing on that minimal aspect seems grotesque as there are much bigger challenges in making potentially large amounts of content actually manageable by a potentially very heterogeneous group of content creators with varying skills, responsibilities and relationships.
在 HN 中查看
SunshineTheCat 2026/04/02 01:19
然而，我认为这是事实，当涉及到与我合作过的非编码客户时，他们确实喜欢对具有 UI 的网站进行细微编辑的能力，而不必不断地向开发人员询问。

WordPress 的问题（看起来这个解决方案很大程度上只是复制了这个问题）是它太麻烦和臃肿。

它确实不同于任何 SaaS 或一般软件的现代 UI。

它充满了毫无意义的管理通知，侧边栏有 5 英里长，用户看到的大约 98% 对他们来说毫无意义。

为客户创建一个非常轻量级、最小的 UI，以准确编辑他们需要的内容，或者像您所说的那样，在大多数情况下，仅静态文件确实是最佳解决方案。 “页面构建器”总是变成一场噩梦，客户最终还是将其交给开发人员来“修复”。

不知道为什么这么多人觉得有必要继续使用 WordPress 几十年来积累的臃肿和粗糙，即使它是“现代化的”。

I think this is true, however, when it comes to non-coding clients I've worked with they really do like the ability to make minor edits to a site with a UI rather than having to continually ping a developer.

The problem with WordPress (and it looks like this solution largely just replicated the problem) is that it's way too cumbersome and bloated.

It really is unlike any modern UI for really any SaaS or software in general.

It's filled with meaningless admin notices, the sidebar is 5 miles long and about 98% of what the user sees is meaningless to them.

Creating a very lightweight, minimal UI for the client to edit exactly what they need or like you said, just static files really is the best solution in most cases. The "page builders" always just turn into a nightmare the clients end up handing over for a dev to "fix" anyways.

Not sure why so many people feel the need to continue on the decades of bloat and cruft WordPress has accumulated, even if it's "modernized."

在 HN 中查看
yurishimo 2026/04/02 05:06
作为在该生态系统中开始从事 webdev 的人，从我的角度来看，WordPress 网站有两种类型。

第一个可以说是最大的正是您所描述的。适合小型企业的小型网站，这些企业只想在线展示，并可能通过小型网上商店或论坛促进一些轻型业务的发展。这些网站是由夜间营销人员完成的，他们也在 Facebook 上兜售 SEO 优化和广告，他们会以 100 美元/月的超低价格托管​​您的网站，同时避开您的电话，而 Godaddy 每月 5 美元的计划实际上是在把您的网站托管在床上。

第二个也是更有影响力的 WordPress 用户群体是非常大的组织，他们发布大量内容，需要灵活、可合理扩展且雇用开发人员成本低廉的内容。大学喜欢 WP，因为他们可以设置多站点，并为每个班级的每个学生提供一个带有一些基本插件的网站，然后就可以了。查看 WordPress VIP 的徽标列表，了解哪些媒体组织由 WP 提供支持。合法的新闻编辑室主要在 WP 后端上运行，但有自己的设计师和一些自定义的发布工作流程。

这两个细分市场相距甚远，但从许多不同角度造成了许多分歧和摩擦。您是否迎合小型企业并接受他们有一天会超越该平台？或者你是否开发了让大型发行商满意的东西，因为大多数从事开源项目的工程人才的工资更普遍？所有这一切同时保持向后兼容性并在某种程度上试图跟上现代实践（毕竟他们确实采用了 React）。

WordPress 很奇怪，我想我想说的绝不是单一文化。

There are two types of WordPress sites from my perspective as someone who got their start in webdev in that ecosystem.

The first and arguably largest is exactly what you describe. Little sites for small businesses who just want an online presence and maybe to facilitate some light duty business development with a small webshop or forum. These sites are done by fly by night marketers who are also hawking SEO optimization and ads on Facebook and they’ll host your site for the low low price of $100/mo while dodging your phone calls when the godaddy $5/mo plan they are actually hosting your site on shits the bed.

The second, and more influential group of WordPress users, are very large organizations who publish a lot of content and need something that is flexible, reasonably scalable and cheap to hire developers for. Universities love WP because they can setup multisite and give every student in every class a website with some basic plugins and then it’s handsoff. Go look at the logo list for WordPress VIP to see what media organizations are powered by WP. Legit newsrooms run on mostly stock WP backends but with their own designers and some custom publishing workflows.

These two market segments are so far apart though that it creates a lot of division and friction from lots of different angles. Do you cater to the small businesses and just accept that they’ll outgrow the platform someday? Or do you build stuff that makes the big publishers happy because the pay for most of the engineering talent working on the open source project more generally? And all that while maintaining backwards compatibility and somewhat trying to keep up with modern-ish practices (they did adopt React after all).

WordPress is weird and in no way a monoculture is what I guess I’m trying to say.

在 HN 中查看
transcriptase 2026/04/02 11:03
我不知道这是否仍然正确，但过去的情况是，安装 WordPress 时你有 3 个选择，甚至还有几个插件：

1）兼职更新它和插件，确保你没有在每一步引入漏洞

2) 保持原样，希望您的特定版本或插件版本不会发现漏洞

3）让东西自动更新并祈祷你的插件不会被出售或泄露并在你的网站上留下后门

I have no idea if it’s still true but it used to be the case that you had 3 choices with a Wordpress install and even a couple plugins:

1) Have a part time job updating it and plugins, making sure you weren’t introducing vulns at every step

2) Leave it as is and hope that no vulns are discovered for your particular version or plugin versions

3) Have things auto-update and pray that your plugins don't get sold or compromised and backdoor your site

在 HN 中查看
wolrah 2026/04/02 12:46
4）如果必须使用任何插件，请不要使用一堆插件，尽可能让它们保持愚蠢并坚持使用那些具有长期声誉的插件。

设置为自动更新的基本实例安装在共享网络主机上，其中操作系统/网络服务器更新是其他人的问题，这是非常万无一失的。 运行设置为自动更新的长期发行版的 VPS 几乎同样好。

---

也就是说，几年前我个人放弃了用于静态站点生成的 Wordpress，因为我意识到我实际上不需要任何动态功能，并且没有使用所见即所得编辑器。 现在，我将 Markdown 写入 git 存储库中的文件中，然后每当更新它时就会触发重新生成。

4) Don't use a stack of plugins, if you must use any keep them as dumb as possible and stick to those with a longstanding reputation.

A basic instance, set to auto-update, installed on a shared webhost where OS/web server updates are someone else's problem is pretty foolproof. A VPS running a long-term distro set to auto update is almost as good.

---

That said I personally dropped Wordpress for static site generation years ago because I realized I didn't actually need any of the dynamic features and wasn't using the WYSIWYG editor. Now I write Markdown in to a file in a git repo and then trigger a regeneration whenever I update it.

ehnto 2026/04/02 10:54
我编写了自己的 CMS，因为 WordPress 的核心功能并没有太多需要复制的地方。

但最终 WordPress 生态系统太强大了，真正的价值主张是插件和熟悉度。直到今天仍然如此，这就是为什么尽管 WordPress 的用户体验、架构和开发人员体验明显更好，但没有任何 CMS 能够取代 WordPress。当客户拥有一套已经使用了 10 多年、现在已成为其业务核心的插件时，这一切都无关紧要。

I wrote my own CMS, as the core WordPress functionality wasn't too much to replicate.

But eventually the WordPress ecosystem was too strong, and the real value proposition was plugins and familiarity. That continues to be true to this day, which is why no CMS has de-throned WordPress in spite of significantly better UX, architecture and developer experience. None of it matters when the client has a suite of plugins they have been using for 10+ years, that are now core to their business.

tootie 2026/04/02 01:39
但“回归 CMS 根源”绝对不是 WordPress 生态系统的目的。它是关于绝对的插件星系，为您提供“盒子里”的完整数字体验。您只需安装电子商务、CRM、表单管理、支付、活动日历的任何插件。它们都将插件到模板系统和 MySQL 数据库。有很多知名且信誉良好的插件，拥有庞大的安装基础（woocommerce、gravity forms、yoast seo），但也有大量可疑的插件可能会感染您的安装。 Cloudflare 正在直接解决现有插件架构的缺点，表明他们打算让 EmDash 填补类似的利基市场，作为一体化数字体验，而不仅仅是简单的 CMS。
But "back to CMS roots" is absolutely not what the WordPress ecosystem is about. It's about the absolute galaxy of plugins that provide you with an entire digital experience "in a box". You can just install whatever plugins for ecommerce, CRM, forms management, payments, event calendars. They will all plugin to both the template system and the MySQL database. There are a lot of well-known and reputable plugins with huge installed bases (woocommerce, gravity forms, yoast seo) but there's a ton of shady ones that can infect your install. Cloudflare is directly addressing the shortcomings of the existing plugin architecture indicating they intend for EmDash to fill a similar niche as an All-in-One digital experience and not just a simple CMS.

The question is then they'd be building some brand new thing not compatible with wordpress. Supposedly the proposition is to steal people away from wordpress. Not just get people building something from scratch looking for a new framework. I'm guessing the recent lawsuits also provide some momentum.

eloisant 2026/04/02 03:45
网站过去都是静态 html 文件。

您可以手动编写它们，也可以使用本地生成的工具，上传所有内容，然后就完成了。完美的安全保障。精彩的表演。

从这个意义上说，静态生成器回到源头，只是生成愚蠢的 HTML 文件，您将其上传/发布到不需要运行任何代码的 Web 服务器。只是提供文件。

Websites used to be static html files.

You either write them by hand, or use a tool that generates it locally, upload everything and you're done. Perfect security. Great performances.

It's in this sense that static generators go back to the source, the simply produce dumb HTML files that you upload/publish to a web server that doesn't need to run any code. Just serve files.

在 HN 中查看
omnimus 2026/04/02 05:57
恕我直言，CMS只是一个在服务器上生成静态html文件的工具。这种区别有点人为。 CMS 具有静态 html 缓存，CDN 将允许您“一键”防火墙动态管理并为您缓存静态 html。

静态网站生成器对于程序员来说是在他们的机器上完成这项工作的很酷的方式，但最终所提供的服务的区别非常小（如果您设置了基础知识）。

Imho CMS is just a tool that generates static html files on the server. The distinction is a bit artificial. CMSes have static html cashing and CDNs will allow you to "one-click" firewall the dynamic administration and cache the static html for you.

Static website generators are cool way for programmers to do that work on their machine but in the end the distinction of what gets served is very small (if you set up the basics).

在 HN 中查看
linkjuice4all 2026/04/02 08:32
CMS 允许非技术人员更新网站 - 这就是为什么 WordPress、Drupal 以及所有“数字体验平台”的摇摇欲坠的尸体仍然像他们一样控制着金钱和眼球。

继续让您的内容人员访问静态网站构建器，看看这个过程崩溃的速度有多快。静态站点生成器对于工程师来说是完美的，但对于营销人员来说却很糟糕，他们是面向公众的网站的实际“客户”。

CMSs allow non-technical people to update the site - that's why WordPress, Drupal, and all of the shambling corpses of "digital experience platforms" still command the dollars and eyeballs that they do.

Go ahead and give your content people access to a static site builder and see how quickly the process falls apart. Static site generators are perfect for engineers but terrible for the marketing people that are the actual "customers" of your public-facing website.

在 HN 中查看
marcus_holmes 2026/04/02 10:46
我这样做了，你是100%正确的。

我使用 Hugo，告诉营销人员向我发送一个 Markdown 文件，然后我将其加载到 Hugo。这对他们来说显然太痛苦了。所以我告诉他们给我发一份 Word 文档，我将其转换为 Markdown 并加载。那太痛苦了。我告诉他们给我发一封包含文字和图像的电子邮件，然后我会解决其余的问题。那太痛苦了。

他们找了一些营销机构在 Wordpress 中重写了整个营销网站，然后我们不得不实施一些可怕的组装才能让我们的后端重定向到他们糟糕的 WP 主机以获取适当的页面。太糟糕了。

但营销人员终于高兴了。他们可以在实际的 CMS 中自己编写一篇博客文章（没有人阅读），并在按下按钮时看到它上线。

我们在一家资金短缺的初创公司花了数千美元来处理这些废话。

I did this, and you are 100% correct.

I used Hugo, told the marketing people to send me a markdown file and I'd load it up to Hugo. That was clearly too painful for them. So I told them to send me a Word doc and I'd convert it to markdown and load it up. That was too painful. I told them to send me an email with the words and images and I'd work out the rest. That was too painful.

They got some marketing agency to rewrite the entire marketing site in Wordpress, and then we had to implement some godawful kludges to get our backend to redirect to their shitty WP host for the appropriate pages. It was awful.

But the marketing folks were finally happy. They could write a blog post (that no-one read) themselves in the actual CMS and see it go live when they pushed the button.

We spent thousands, in a cash-strapped startup, dealing with this bullshit.

在 HN 中查看
earthlingdavey 2026/04/02 04:29
这很有趣。我断断续续地使用 WordPress 已经有 10 年了，我确信这个项目有两点绝对正确。 TypeScript 和 Worker 插件。

我最近对 ​​WP 的安全性或缺乏性进行了很多思考。在 WP 中，恶意插件可以访问数据库、环境变量、在屏幕上呈现文本（例如 XSS）。幸运的是，精心设计的插件系统可以缓解所有这些问题。

我在业余时间一直致力于开发一个无头 CMS，它在某些方面与 EmDash 非常相似。它还处于非常早期的开发阶段，但无论如何我都会分享。它叫做 HotsauceCMS - https://github.com/hotsauce-team/hotsauce

- 我使用了可选的 NodeJS 或 Deno Worker 插件，这意味着第一方插件可以受益于进程内的速度，而其他插件可以在 Workers 中运行。对于细粒度的权限控制，您可以使用 Deno Workers。

- 我的依赖绝对最小，我厌倦了 Dependabot 警报和 npm 供应链黑客。我的 CMS 只有 4 个依赖项，0 个传递依赖项。

- 首先是 Drizzle 模式，而且是无头的。因此，您可以完全控制数据库结构，在架构中使用 cms 提示来实现文件上传等功能。

- 它与数据库无关，因此它适用于任何 Drizzle 支持的数据库（Postgres、MySQL、SQLite）

- 由于是无头的，你可以使用任何前端，我更喜欢 JSX 不带反应，但一切都可以。

HotsauceCMS 绝对欢迎反馈，我是否错过了一个技巧，我是否走在正确的轨道上？

不管怎样，恭喜 EmDash。我将密切关注，很高兴看到接下来几个月的进展。

This is very interesting. I've worked with WordPress on and off for 10 years, and I'm convinced that this project has got 2 things absolutely spot on. TypeScript and Worker plugins.

I've given the security, or lack of, WP a lot of thought recently. In WP malicious plugin has access to the database, enfironment variables, rendering text on screen (think XSS). Luckily, a thoughtfully designed plugin system can mitigate all of those issues.

I've been working on a headless CMS in my spare time that is eirily similar to EmDash in a few ways. It's in very early development, but I will share regardless. It's called HotsauceCMS - https://github.com/hotsauce-team/hotsauce

- I went with optional NodeJS or Deno Worker plugins, this means that first-party plugins can benefit from the speed of in-process, and other plugins can be run in Workers. For fine grained permission control, you can use Deno Workers.

- I went with absolute minimal dependencies, I am so fed up with Dependabot alerts and npm supply chain hacks. My CMS has only 4 dependencies, 0 transistive dependencies.

- It's Drizzle schema first, and headless. So you have full controll of the database structure, use cms hints in your schema for features like file upload.

- It's database-agnostic, so it works with any Drizzle-supported database (Postgres, MySQL, SQLite)

- Being headless, you can use any frontend, my preference is JSX w/o react, but anything goes.

Feedback is absolutely welcomed on HotsauceCMS, did I miss a trick, am I on the right track?

Anyway, congratulations on EmDash. I'll be following closely, excited to see how the next few months unfold.

在 HN 中查看
mi_lk 2026/04/02 05:27
> 我确信这个项目有两点绝对正确。 TypeScript 和 Worker 插件。

你能解释一下为什么 TS 如此出色吗？

> I'm convinced that this project has got 2 things absolutely spot on. TypeScript and Worker plugins.

Can you explain why TS is spot on?

在 HN 中查看
earthlingdavey 2026/04/02 06:18
最主要的是统一类型。

- 我已经完成了 GraphQL 服务器的构建步骤，以在语言之间共享类型。

- 我使用了非类型化的 JS 客户端代码。

两者都容易出现错误，而且没有太多乐趣。

前端和后端的 TS：共享类型意味着您将拥有编辑器类型提示、在 lint（或构建）时捕获类型错误，甚至可以在客户端和浏览器之间共享验证逻辑。

The main thing is unified types.

- I've been done GraphQL server with a build step to share types between languages.

- I've used untyped JS client side code.

Both are prone to bugs, and not much fun.

TS for front and back end: sharing types means you'll have editor type hints, catch type errors at lint (or build), and you might even share validation logic between client and browser.

I personally i am in the other camp and think they got the language change absolutley wrong. I already have php installed on my server why do i need to install TypeScript? The easiest website to run is in php. All other languages add a barrier.

I used to work with Wordpress a lot, but last time was a couple years ago.

The appeal for the company i was working with was ease of installation on legacy servers (FTP). You would just upload the files, and it worked. No CLI tools, no dependency management, no build tools.

But yeah, security was a big issue. Constant hacks.

在 HN 中查看
omnimus 2026/04/02 05:42
不断的黑客攻击是 WordPress 流行的副作用。每一个发现的安全漏洞几乎都会立即被机器人利用。除非你跟上更新，否则你很容易受到攻击。 这并不是因为 WordPress 是建立在“遗留”堆栈上的。该堆栈上的其他 CMS（许多都非常流行）不存在此问题。
The constant hacks are side effect of Wordpress popularity. Every discovered security flaw is exploited by bots almost immediately. Unless you keep up with the updates you are very vulnerable. It is not because wordpress is built on "legacy" stack. Other CMSes on that stack (and many are very popular) don't have this problem.
在 HN 中查看
chrismorgan 2026/04/02 09:00
受欢迎程度有所帮助，但这也是因为 WordPress 的安全模型是疯狂的。 PHP 比大多数语言更容易实现这种疯狂，WordPress 接受了这一点，而 Drupal 之类的人则拒绝这样做。
The popularity helps, but it’s also because WordPress’s security model is distilled insanity. PHP makes this insanity far easier than most languages, and WordPress embraces that, whereas the likes of Drupal rebuff it.

donohoe 2026/04/02 10:24
我认为安全问题在于人们信任随机插件而不审查它们。

我一直在小型和大型公司中运行 WP，没有出现大的安全问题。您可以构建自己的插件，也可以使用您需要的少数值得信赖的插件来增强您的操作。

I think the security issue is that people trust random plugins without reviewing them.

I’ve been running WP with small and large companies and no big security issues. You either build your own plugins or go with the trusted few you need to augment your operation.

dominotw 2026/04/02 08:52
https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47503348
https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47503348
在 HN 中查看
rcarr 2026/04/02 06:51
在我看来，Cloudflare 的角度是错误的。 WordPress 之所以如此受欢迎，是因为在当时它是构建网站最简单的方法。因此，它背后的工程师产生了网络效应，这就是为什么它在今天 40% 的网站上持续存在的原因。 React 也发生了同样的事情 - 大多数 Typescript 站点都是用 React 和 NextJS 编写的，因为它周围的网络效应。

是的，安全方面很重要，但是到目前为止，有多少 WordPress 工程师在已经接受了风险的情况下，会因为安全而跳槽到这一点呢？我的钱不多。如果某人是 2026 年的 WordPress 开发人员，他们可能不是那种喜欢提高技能和学习新技术的开发人员。同样，如果您希望针对想要建立新网站的普通人，那么该消费者真的会选择这个而不是 Wix 或 Squarespace 吗？它看起来并不容易使用，所以我不会指望它。那么网络效应从何而来，使其成为新的 WordPress？

如果他们坚持下去，我可以看到 Vinext 取得成功——我认为有相当多的人想要离开 Vercel（当生态系统更加稳定时，他们可能会迁移到 Tanstack）。但我不确定 WordPress 上的人们是否真的想离开。如果他们真的想取得成功，我认为他们需要一个更好的角度，在我看来，这将使其比 Squarespace/Wix/Shopify 等更容易、更快、更便宜、更灵活

In my opinion, Cloudflare are coming at this from the wrong angle. WordPress is so popular because back in the day it was the easiest way to get a website built. So it got a network effect of engineers behind it which is why it persists at 40% of websites today. Same thing happened with React - majority of Typescript sites are written in React and NextJS because of the network effect around it.

Yeah the security aspect is important, but how many of those Wordpress engineers are going to jump ship to this because of security when they've been fine with the risk so far? My money is not a lot. If someone is a WordPress dev in 2026, they're probably not the type of dev that likes to upskill and learn new tech. Similarly, if you're looking to target the average joe looking to build a fresh website, would that consumer really choose this over Wix or Squarespace? It doesn't look easier to use so I wouldn't count on it. So where is the network effect going to come from to make this the new WordPress?

I could see Vinext being successful if they keep at it— I think there are a sizeable amount of people who would like to move away from Vercel (and who will probably migrate to Tanstack when the ecosystem is more stable). But I'm not sure people on WordPress really want to leave. If they really want to make this successful I think they need a better angle which in my opinion would be making it easier, quicker, cheaper and more flexible than Squarespace/Wix/Shopify etc

在 HN 中查看
foopod 2026/04/02 03:54
作为一名（不幸的是）wordpress 开发人员，这似乎解决了我使用 WP 的最大痛点。这不是插件安全性，而是整体插件架构。

WP 将插件视为内容，实际上与上传的图像位于同一顶级 `wp-content` 目录中。这使得 CI/CD 成为一场噩梦。但 EmDash 插件只是 TS 模块，即使插件配置最终出现在数据库中的某个地方，这也必须让事情变得更容易。

As a (unfortunately) wordpress dev this seems to solve my single biggest painpoint with WP. Which isn't plugin security, but the overall plugin architecture.

WP treats plugins as content, literally in the same top level `wp-content` directory as uploaded images. This makes CI/CD among other things, a nightmare. But EmDash plugins are just TS modules, which has got to make things easier even if plugin configuration does end up in the db somewhere.

bombcar 2026/04/02 05:06
WordPress 没有“临时站点”的概念，也没有办法进行更改，然后将它们从开发“导出”到生产；您基本上必须将其恢复为备份，或者只是手动重播更改。
Wordpress has no concept of a "staging site" and no way to make changed and then "export" them from dev to production; you basically have to either restore it as a backup or just replay the changes by hand.

andy_xor_andrew 2026/04/02 01:01
> x402 是一种开放、中立的互联网原生支付标准。它让互联网上的任何人都可以轻松收费，并且任何客户都可以按使用付费的方式按需付费。客户端（例如代理）发送 HTTP 请求并接收 HTTP 402 需要付款状态代码。作为响应，客户端为按需访问付费，服务器可以让客户端访问所请求的内容。

迷人。 Cloudflare 设想未来代理商将获得其所有者的借记卡，这样他们就可以自主向网站所有者发送微交易，以抓取内容或可能代表所有者购买商品。我不知道我对此有何感想，但毫无疑问这是一个令人着迷的概念。

Brb，设置一个蜜罐，总是以 HTTP 402 Payment required 要求每次访问 10 美分......我猜这就是下一个“在我的网站上以每个 1 美元的价格出售 100 万像素”

> x402 is an open, neutral standard for Internet-native payments. It lets anyone on the Internet easily charge, and any client pay on-demand, on a pay-per-use basis. A client, such as an agent, sends a HTTP request and receives a HTTP 402 Payment Required status code. In response, the client pays for access on-demand, and the server can let the client through to the requested content.

Fascinating. Cloudflare is envisioning a future where agents are given debit cards by their owners, so they can autonomously send microtransactions to website owners to scrape content or possibly purchase goods on the owner's behalf. I don't know how I feel about that but there's no doubt it's a fascinating concept.

Brb, setting up a honeypot that always responds with HTTP 402 Payment Required demanding 10cents per visit... That's the next "selling 1 million pixels on my website for $1 each", I guess

在 HN 中查看
NBJack 2026/04/02 10:40
坦白说，我认为这是一个精心设计的愚人节玩笑，尤其是当我看到这一点时。它向我建议“你可以收取任何费用”，同时巧妙地暗示你也应该向他们付费（每隔几段就在一个方便的部署按钮内），以获得在他们的托管解决方案上运行他们的东西的特权。

我想这就是我们生活的世界。

To be frank, I thought this was an elaborate April Fool's joke, particularly when I saw that. It suggested to me 'you could charge for anything' while subtly implying you should also be paying them (within a handy deploy button every few paragraphs) for the privilege of running their stuff on their hosting solution.

I suppose this is the world we live in.

benatkin 2026/04/02 01:14
这是一个合法的愚人节。

在初始提交时：

> 部分内容被隐藏

> 大型提交默认隐藏一些内容。使用下面的搜索框查找可能隐藏的内容。

这是“WordPress 的精神继承者”。

It's a legit April Fools'.

On the initial commit:

> Some content is hidden

> Large Commits have some content hidden by default. Use the searchbox below for content that may be hidden.

This for "a spiritual successor to WordPress".

I am not a FOMO victim.

在 HN 中查看
sophacles 2026/04/02 05:48
在 20 世纪 90 年代和 2000 年代早期，有一些年有一些很好的愚人节笑话，这就是泡沫。不是每个人都这样做，所以新颖性也让“meh”的看起来更好。到了 2008 年左右，每个人都在做这样的事情，而且大多数都不是很好。到了 2012 年，营销活动开始介入，几乎所有的营销活动都非常糟糕且无趣。

这是一个很好的传统，但和许多事情一样，这个场景变得太大、太企业化。有一段时间这是僵尸传统，然后慢慢消失了。

事实上，当 cloudflare 在 4 月 1 日开始发布严肃的内容时，我发现这是对这一比喻的令人耳目一新的颠覆。

There were some years in the 90s and early 2ks that had good april fool's jokes, and that was what bubbled up. Not everyone did, so the novelty also made the "meh" ones seem better. By 2008ish everyone was doing one, and most of them weren't very good. By 2012ish marketing got involved and almost all of them were terrible and unfunny.

It was a nice tradition but, like many things, the scene got too big and corporate. It was a zombie tradition for a while then slowly faded away.

In fact when cloudflare started releasing serious things on 4/1, I found it to be a refreshing subversion of the trope.

在 HN 中查看
8organicbits 2026/04/02 01:30
我认为你需要考虑基本费率。有很多 WordPress 插件漏洞被披露，因为 WordPress 插件非常多，而且插件的部署也足够多，因此搜索这些漏洞是值得的。

该网站警告说 WordPress 插件可以被放弃，但这显然不是 WordPress 特有的问题。当然，有些网站可以使用 SSG，但这是不同的设计。

我当然不想声称 WordPress 安全性很好，但我不确定该网站是否衡量了任何有意义的事情。

I think you need to account for the base rate. There's a lot of WordPress plugin vulnerability disclosures because there's a lot of WordPress plugins and there are enough deployments of the plugins to make searching for those vulnerabilities is worthwhile.

That site warns that WordPress plugins can be abandoned, but that's clearly not a WordPress specific issue. Sure some site could use SSG, but that's a different design.

I certainly don't want to claim WordPress security is good, but I'm not sure that site is measuring anything meaningful.

zdragnar 2026/04/02 01:21
它将插件作为字符串存储在数据库中，然后将这些字符串拉回并根据请求将它们评估为 PHP。

“更好的编码”在很大程度上是一种主观评估。

It stores plugins as strings in the database, then pulls those strings back and evals them as PHP on requests.

"Better coded" is very much a subjective assessment.

在 HN 中查看
coldtea 2026/04/02 07:34
如果它为 30-50% 的网络（包括数千个主要网站）提供支持，那么它在某种程度上是有效的。

象牙塔“只是不要使用低成本的解决方案”，人们不会把钱交给人们去使用成本更高的解决方案，不是吗？

除了草率之外，忽略它的使用原因意味着他们对人们真正想要的东西有一个巨大的盲点：

“wordpress 很有价值，因为它允许非常糟糕的开发人员/营销人员编写非常糟糕的代码并侥幸逃脱，为具有成本意识的客户提供极低成本的解决方案”

这句话中没有任何内容没有描述非常真实的需求。

If it powers 30-50% of the web, including thousands of major websites, it works at some level.

Ivory tower "just don't use a low-cost solution" people aren't going to hand over money to people to use a higher-cost one, are they?

And ignoring why it's used besides the sloppiness means they have a huge blind spot to what people actually want:

"wordpress is valuable because it allows very bad developers / marketing people to write very bad code and get away with it, driving extremely low cost solutions for clients who are cost concious"

Nothing in this quote doesn't describe very real needs.

在 HN 中查看
busterarm 2026/04/02 01:35
> wordpress 很有价值，因为它允许非常糟糕的开发人员/营销人员编写非常糟糕的代码并侥幸逃脱，为注重成本的客户提供极低成本的解决方案。

你已经成功了，但这并不是一件坏事。 对于这些客户来说，不存在替代方案。

还有另一个垂直领域，即拥有大量作家编写内容的组织。基本上任何类型的出版商或广告商。 没有比这更好的 CMS 了。 像《纽约时报》等大型组织选择自己编写。

> wordpress is valuable because it allows very bad developers / marketing people to write very bad code and get away with it, driving extremely low cost solutions for clients who are cost concious.

You've sort of nailed it, but this isn't a bad thing. An alternative for these customers does not exist.

There's another vertical which is organizations that have armies of writers churning out content. Any kind of publisher or advertiser, basically. There is no better CMS for this. Large organizations like NYT, etc chose to write their own.

在 HN 中查看
QuantumGood 2026/04/02 03:53
我在 90 年代末开始为客户构建网站，并很快将“客户可以在所有页面上编辑他们的电话号码”作为关键要求。 Wordpress 的所见即所得页面构建器解决了这个问题——它不是唯一的解决方案，但它的工作方式非常接近开箱即用。
I started building sites for clients in the late '90's, and quickly made "client can edit their phone number on all pages" a key requirement. Wordpress with a WYSIWYG page builder solves that — it's not the only solution, but it works pretty close to right out of the box.
在 HN 中查看
busterarm 2026/04/02 02:41
这也让您不必 24/7 待命。

十年前，我为了一份工作必须学习并运行 WordPress。 我捂住鼻子，臭气熏天。 但很快我就学会了如何管理它并围绕它进行现代合理的实践，并且我可能比我接触过的任何其他 CMS 或 Web 框架都从中获得了更多的实际价值。 其中包括 Rails。

值得庆幸的是，我不必再这样做了，但今天你可以理智、安全地运行 WordPress，而且这一切都是零耻辱。

Which also allows you to not be on call 24/7.

A decade ago I had to learn and run WordPress for a job. I held my nose up the stink was so bad. But quickly I learned how to manage it and have modern sensible practices around it and I've probably gotten more real value out of it than any other CMS or web framework I've touched. That includes Rails.

Thankfully I don't have to do that anymore, but you can sanely and safely run WordPress today and there's zero shame in it.

在 HN 中查看
bombcar 2026/04/02 02:47
有些选项可以由任何人运行，但它们可以执行的操作和展示的内容通常非常有限。

WordPress 坚定地处于中间立场，如果有人愿意付费，你可以进行大量的定制，然后他们可以进行日常维护和喂养。

其他一切要么在所有可能的方面都变得更糟（Joomla！），要么成为任何人都无法使用的开发人员愿望清单的集合（Drupal）。

There are options that can be run by anyone, but they're often very constrained in what they can do and show.

Wordpress is solidly in that middle ground where you can do a large amount of customization if someone'll pay for it, and then they can do the day-to-day care and feeding of it.

Everything else has either been much worse in all possible ways (Joomla!) or has been a collection of developer wish-lists unusable by anyone (Drupal).

我曾经这样做过，在 Fiverr 上雇了一个人做一个 WordPress 网站。他们无缘无故地安装了一大堆插件，弄得一团糟，然后把钱退给了我。我回到静态站点。

这是我的经验，进入门槛低，价格低，做工粗制滥造。或者聘请一家代理机构，花大钱做很少的工作。

I did that once, employed someone on Fiverr to do a WordPress site. They installed a load of plugins for no reason, made a mess, then gave me my money back. I went back to a static site.

That has been my experience, low barrier to entry, low price, shoddy work. Or hire an agency, pay top dollar for little work.

gbibas 2026/04/02 09:47
哈，是的，这就是它的另一面——低进入门槛是双向的。 WordPress 人才库巨大但未经过滤。尽管如此，池的存在才是 WordPress 保持主导地位的原因。目前还没有人雇佣 Fiverr 来定制 EmDash 网站。
Ha, yeah that's the other side of it — low barrier to entry cuts both ways. The WordPress talent pool is huge but unfiltered. Still, the fact that pool exists at all is what keeps WordPress dominant. Nobody's hiring Fiverr gigs to customize an EmDash site yet.

0xbadcafebee 2026/04/02 02:01
严肃的问题：为什么每个人仍然使用 JavaScript 来进行 AI 编码项目？您现在可以使用真实语言对应用程序进行振动编码。

没有理由再使用解释性的、臃肿的、奇怪的语言了。解释语言存在的唯一原因是您可以编辑文件并立即重新运行它，而无需编译步骤。编译现在很便宜，并且您不再需要使用新语言来构建专业知识。要求 AI 用 Go 编写你的应用程序，它会很乐意遵守。运行它，速度更快，内存使用和磁盘空间更少。代码更简单、更小，使得审查更容易。分发就像“复制文件”一样简单。

我同意，解释语言跳过“可移植性”编译/分发步骤，让您避免愚蠢的 MacOS 代码签名。但是 Go 很容易交叉编译，而且（afaik？）用户可以很容易地取消隔离自签名应用程序。

Serious question: Why is everyone still using JavaScript to AI-code projects? You can vibe-code apps with real languages now.

There's no reason to use an interpreted, bloated, weird language anymore. The only reason interpreted languages were a thing was so you could edit a file and re-run it immediately without a compile step. Compiling is now cheap, and you don't have to build expertise in a new language anymore. Ask AI to write your app in Go, it'll happily comply. Run it and it's faster with less memory use and disk space. The code is simpler and smaller making reviewing easier. Distribution is as easy as "copy the file".

I'll grant you, interpreted languages skip the "portability" compiling/distributing step, and let you avoid the stupid MacOS code signing. But Go is stupid easy to cross-compile, and (afaik?) the user can un-quarantine a self-signed app pretty easily.

在 HN 中查看
0xbadcafebee 2026/04/02 06:33
Typescript 确实很奇怪。您从 Javascript 开始，这是一种用于验证 HTML 表单的语言。然后，您实现一个字节码编译器，这样它就不会变得异常缓慢且有错误。然后你实现“框架”，尝试让它在疯狂的网络浏览器平台上创建真正的应用程序。为了避免学习或创造一种新语言而把自己扭曲成椒盐卷饼之后，你最终在第一种语言之上发明了另一种语言，这样你就可以拥有类型。最重要的是，它占用了大量的内存和磁盘空间。 （我没有提到速度慢，因为我认为这是理所当然的，但比 Go 代码慢 5 倍到 10 倍仍然值得考虑）
Typescript is legitimately weird. You start off with Javascript, a language made to validate HTML forms. Then you implement a bytecode compiler so it's not unusably slow and buggy. Then you implement "frameworks" to try to make it create real applications across the insane platform that is the web browser. After twisting yourself into a pretzel just to avoid learning or making a new language, you finally invent another language on top of the first one just so you can have types. To top it all off, it takes up tons of memory and disk space. (I don't mention the slowness because I take it for granted, but 5x-10x slower than Go code is still worth considering)
在 HN 中查看
znort_ 2026/04/02 08:50
我也不喜欢 ts，但可以看到它对商业的吸引力。当然，它是用螺栓固定的……就像网络上的几乎所有东西一样，这是一个完整的弗兰肯斯坦。但它确实有效，而且事实是目前还没有其他选择。 otoh 如今，它的性能和效率几乎足以应对任何事情。

在 go（或其他）中编码和转译只是在此之上的额外步骤。并非没有好处，介意！当然，在很多情况下这是适当的。但这仍然并不意味着它是普遍的“正确的事情”。这让我想到：

> 只是为了避免学习或创造一门新语言

根本不存在“一种语言可以统治所有语言”，也不应该有一种语言。我的整个职业生涯都是关于学习不同的语言、技术、工具、系统、环境和工作流程。很多，几乎不间断。 javascript 有它好的地方，我喜欢它的香草，因为它的怪异和毫无歉意的无类型性。 :)

i'm not a fan of ts either, but can see the appeal for business. of course it is bolted on ... like almost everything on the web, which is a complete frankenstein. but it works, and truth is there is no other alternative yet. otoh it is performant and efficient enough for almost anything these days.

coding in go (or whatever) and transpiling is just an extra step on top of that. not without benefits, mind! there are surely many situations where that is appropriate. but that still doesn't mean it is universally the "right thing". which brings me to:

> just to avoid learning or making a new language

there simply is no "one language to rule them all" nor there ought to be one. my entire career has been about learning different languages, techniques, tools, systems, environments and workflows. lots of them, nearly non stop. javascript has its nice place, and i like it vanilla, in all its weirdness and unapologetic untypedness. :)

在 HN 中查看
dgb23 2026/04/02 05:52
Go 的具体问题在于，它不太适合处理任意 JSON ，尤其是典型的前端代码。

一个问题是您不编写 Go 代码，而是编写 Go 加上一些模板语言（例如 html/template 或 go templ）。无法无缝地从常规代码和模板代码迁移会增加摩擦，并在开发、解决问题和迭代时受到限制。

另一个问题是类型系统对于前端代码来说通常表达能力不够。因此，您要么必须生成类型，要么最终得到像 map[string]any 这样的东西，这很尴尬，并且比动态语言通常提供的保证和支持更少（换句话说，动态语言比 Go 更擅长动态语言）。

现在，这些问题并不会出现在每个基于 Web UI 的项目中，但当它们出现时，与过去相比，这确实是一件痛苦且有限的事情。

The problem with Go specifically is that it's not great for processing arbitrary JSON and especially not your typical frontend code.

One issue is that you don't write Go code, you write Go plus some templating language (like html/template or go templ). Not being able to seamlessly move from regular code and template code adds friction and is limiting while developing, figuring stuff out and iterating.

Another problem is that the type system is often not expressive enough for frontend code. So you either have to generate types or end up with something like map[string]any, which is awkward and gives you less guarantees and support than what dynamic languages typically offer (in other words, dynamic languages are better at being dynamic languages than Go).

Now these problems don't emerge in every web UI based project, but when they do, it's really painful and limiting compared to what one is used to.

Yokohiii 2026/04/02 08:44
> 一个问题是你不编写 Go 代码，而是编写 Go 加上一些模板语言（例如 html/template 或 go templ）。无法无缝地从常规代码和模板代码迁移会增加摩擦，并在开发、解决问题和迭代时受到限制。

哇，这是我最近听到的最便宜的借口。模板语言当然可能是狗屎，但要求它成为母语是相当奇怪的。而且，JS 生态系统并没有产生自己奇怪的令人厌恶的东西来进行简单的字符串连接。抱歉，我说得太严厉了，我理解这通常会带来不便，但将 JS 归为优越是大胆的。

> One issue is that you don't write Go code, you write Go plus some templating language (like html/template or go templ). Not being able to seamlessly move from regular code and template code adds friction and is limiting while developing, figuring stuff out and iterating.

Wow that is the cheapest excuse I've heard recently. Templating languages can certainly be dogshit, but demanding it to be the native language is pretty weird. Also it's not like the JS ecosystem spawned their own weird abominations to do simple string concatenation. Sorry for being harsh, I understand that as a general inconvenience but attributing JS as superior is just bold.

Yokohiii 2026/04/02 08:35
大多数程序员都熟悉 JavaScript。 Javascript 拥有最大的社区。如果你想流行，那就 JS 或者更确切地说 TS 吧。

但奇怪的是正确的。只要语言足够主流，这就不重要了。我的假设是，JS/TS 编码人员对切换平台的抵抗力最高，生态系统庞大，跨 BE/FE，性能也不完全是垃圾，他们认识的每个人也都了解该平台。为什么要切换？它是一把螺丝刀，如果你热衷于拧紧东西，你就会这么做。

Most coders are familiar with javascript. Javascript has the largest community. If you want to be popular, then JS or rather TS it is.

But it's correct that it is odd. As long the lang is mainstream enough, it hardly matters. My assumption is that JS/TS coders have the highest resistance to switch platforms, the ecosystem is huge, it's cross BE/FE, performance isn't complete trash, everyone they know knows the platform as well. Why switch? It's a screwdriver, if your passion is to screw things, you will.

在 HN 中查看
theLiminator 2026/04/02 05:38
是的，我的理论是，对于法学硕士来说，Rust 在一段时间内将成为某种“局部最优”。

法学硕士有几个主要问题，他们产生幻觉并犯错误。所以使用它们的理想方式是用形式化方法尽可能地约束它们。 Rust 的类型系统是某些形式的正确性的正式证明，这将使“vibe-coding”比其他语言的工作程度更高。

从长远来看，我怀疑所有的振动编码实际上都会发生在具有依赖类型的语言中，并且我们将尽可能地推动在编译时证明其正确性。由于生成代码的成本正在直线下降，因此代码的绝对数量将呈指数级增长，这是防止无法克服的错误堆积如山的唯一方法。

正式方法和法学硕士是天作之合。

Yeah, my theory is that Rust is going to be a somewhat "local optima" for a while for LLMs.

LLMs have a couple major problem, they hallucinate and make mistakes. So the ideal way to use them is to constrain them as much as possible with formal methods. Rust's type system is a formal proof of some forms of correctness, this will let "vibe-coding" work to a much higher degree than with other languages.

In the very long run, I suspect that all vibe-coding will actually occur in a language with dependent types and we'll push as much as possible into being proven correct at compile-time. Since the cost of generating code is plummeting, and thus the sheer volume of code will be exponentially rising, this is the only way to prevent an unsurmountable mountain of errors.

Formal methods and LLMs are a match made in heaven.

boredtofears 2026/04/02 04:49
LLM 不会从 Javascript/node 等高级语言提供的抽象中受益吗？

也许我说得不够深入，因为我没有做过很多 Golang，但我一直认为它首先是一种系统语言，这意味着你必须自己处理较低级别的问题。我确信有很多库都掩盖了这一点 - 但语言本身的哲学是不同的。 Javascript 的设计目的是很好地解决 CRUD 之类的接口/问题。

也许这只是一个过时的论点，尽管它与现代 golang/rust 并不真正相关。

Do LLM's not benefit from the abstractions higher level languages like Javascript/node offer?

Perhaps I'm speaking out of depth because I haven't done a lot of Golang, but I've always thought of it as a systems language first, which means by necessity you have you to handle lower level problems yourself. I'm sure there's plenty of libraries that paper over this - but the philosophy of the languages themselves is different. Javascript was designed to solve CRUD like interfaces/problems quite well.

Maybe this is just an outdated argument though that isn't really relevant with modern golang/rust though.

在 HN 中查看
0xbadcafebee 2026/04/02 06:06
您可以通过现代法学硕士以任何您想要的流行语言实现应用程序（他们仍然需要接受该语言的培训）。告诉它要写什么，它就会做。但缺点是，如果该语言占用大量内存或占用大量磁盘空间，那么您将不会获得任何好处。如果你不需要自己手动编写代码，那么你不妨选择一种执行时问题最少的语言。

Go 有一个非常可靠的标准库，它删除了许多您通常需要单独实现的内容。你不解决较低级别的问题，因为语言已经解决了它。 Nodejs 有相反的问题，几乎没有标准，所以人们创建了库来实现 5 行代码。 Rust 也有一个最小的标准库，并且比 Go 更复杂。如果你想要一种极其简单、内置电池的编译编程语言，Go 就是它。由于使用 Go 编写的代码较少，因此上下文使用也较少，因此实际上，LLM 使用 Go 应用程序会更轻松。

它最初是一种“系统语言”，但有许多项目扩展了它的用途。有两个独立的框架可让您编写一个 Go 应用程序并将其编译为 Android 应用程序、iOS 应用程序、Mac 应用程序、Windows 应用程序、Linux 应用程序、GUI 和控制台。它有多个 Web 框架，其中一个甚至是 Electron 的替代品。它没有 REPL。

You can implement apps in any popular language you want with modern LLMs (they still need to be trained on that language). Tell it what to write and it'll do it. But the downside is, if the language is a memory hog or takes up tons of disk space, you're not gaining anything. If you don't have to manually write the code yourself, you might as well pick a language that has the fewest problems when executed.

Go has a really solid standard library which removes a lot of what you'd typically implement separately. You don't solve lower level problems because the language already solved it. Nodejs has the opposite issue, where there's virtually nothing standard, so people made libraries to implement 5 lines of code. Rust also has a minimal standard library, and is more complex than Go. If you want a dead-simple, batteries-included compiled programming language, Go is pretty much it. Since you write less code with Go, there's less context use, so actually, the LLM has an easier time with Go apps.

It started as a 'systems language' but it has many projects that extend its usefulness. There are two separate frameworks that let you write one Go app and compile it as an Android app, iOS app, Mac app, Windows app, Linux app, both GUI and console. It has multiple web frameworks, and one is even an Electron replacement. The thing it doesn't have is a REPL.

FlamingMoe 2026/04/02 01:37
由 Cloudflare 支持的 WordPress 精神继承者在理论上听起来很棒，但标题功能（通过 Dynamic Workers 进行插件隔离）仅适用于 Cloudflare 的运行时。在任何其他主机上，它只是一个 TypeScript CMS，没有证明其存在合理性的安全模型。开源但架构锁定。
A WordPress spiritual successor backed by Cloudflare sounds great in theory, but the headline feature, plugin isolation via Dynamic Workers, only works on Cloudflare's runtime. On any other host it's just a TypeScript CMS without the security model that justifies its existence. Open source but architecturally locked in.

amanzi 2026/04/02 05:41
所以这只是一个与 WordPress “类似”的 CMS，因为它有主题和插件，您可以发布页面、帖子、标签、类别等。但是有很多类似的 CMS，而这个与 WordPress 不“兼容”，因为您显然不能只采用 WordPress 主题或插件并将其安装在您的 EmDash 站点中。所以我什至不知道为什么这里关注 WordPress - 这只是另一个提供类似功能的 CMS。
So this is just a "similar" CMS to WordPress in that it has themes and plugins, and you can publish pages, posts, tags, categories, etc. But there are lots of similar CMS out there, and this one isn't "compatible" with WordPress since you obviously can't just take a WordPress theme or plugin and install it in your EmDash site. So I don't even know why the focus on WordPress here - this is just yet another CMS that offers similar features.
在 HN 中查看
alluro2 2026/04/02 07:07
WordPress 是最受欢迎的 CMS，并且在任何时候，尤其是最近，都有很多人在寻找“WP 替代品”。我同意这不是 实际上 兼容，但他们似乎试图让它足够相似，以便能够在没有 100% 撒谎的情况下使用这个词并以这种方式吸引人们。
WordPress is the most popular CMS, and, at any moment in time, especially lately, there are a lot of people looking for a "WP alternative". I agree that it's not actually compatible, but it seems they tried to make it similar just enough to be able to use the word without 100% lying and attract people that way.
在 HN 中查看
smetannik 2026/04/02 09:23
在我看来，与 WP 不同的是，EmDash 更难托管。

通过 WP，您可以找到大量提供预装 WP 的廉价 PHP 主机。 如果您需要调整主题 - 只需通过 FTP 下载 .php 文件，调整它并上传回来。

无需服务器管理或重新启动。

EmDash 的一大潜在优势是每个 WP 部署基本上都是一个蜜罐。

IMO unlike WP, EmDash can he harder to host.

With WP you can find a plethora of cheap PHP hostings that offer WP preinstalled. If you need to tweak a theme - just download a .php file via FTP, tweak it and upload back.

No server management or restart is required.

One big potential benefit that EmDash has - every WP deployment is basically a honeypot.

在 HN 中查看
TheTaytay 2026/04/02 02:59
读完这些评论后，我似乎属于少数派，但我很高兴看到这个公告。

一个“好的”标准、免费的 CMS，具有主题和插件支持，没有 Wordpress 的问题，是_欢迎_。 （问题有很多：许可、信任、戏剧性、安全性和成本）。

我猜这里很多愤世嫉俗的人首先不是 WordPress 的目标市场？您向非技术朋友和家人推荐什么，他们想要一个好的、开源的、价格实惠的 CMS 来支持他们的网站？ Wordpress 的想法都是正确的，但实现却是错误的。

It looks like I'm in the minority after reading this comments, but I'm quite happy to see this announcement.

A "good" standard, free CMS with theming and plugin support without the issues of Wordpress is _welcome_. (And the issues are many: Licensing, trust, drama, security, and cost).

I'm guessing that a lot of cynicism here is coming from this crowd not being the target market of Wordpress in the first place? What were you recommending to non-technical friends and family who wanted a good, open source, affordable CMS to back their website? Wordpress has all the right _ideas_, but the wrong implementation.

在 HN 中查看
9dev 2026/04/02 04:11
那里 是 伟大的标准 CMS，在技术上一切都比 Wordpress 更好（并不是说跳得比岩石更难，但是嘿）。这不是最难的部分。每个开发人员都应该构建一个好的 CMS 一次。

困难的部分是取代 Wordpress 的市场份额；建立一个由博主、营销人员、代理商、网页设计师等组成的社区；创建一个由付费和免费插件组成的庞大生态系统，允许插件开发人员致力于您的市场并锁定客户。

Wordpress 太糟糕了。这 仅有的 它的关键在于它的护城河，但这不是一个工程问题，而是一个人的问题。

There are great standard CMSes that do everything technically better than Wordpress (not that it's harder to jump higher than a rock, but hey). That's not the hard part. Every developer should build a good CMS once.

The hard part is displacing Wordpress market share; building a community of bloggers, marketeers, agencies, web designers, and so on; creating a huge ecosystem of paid and free plugins, allowing plugin devs to commit to your marketplace and lock customers in.

Wordpress is awful. The only thing it's got going is its moat, but that's not an engineering problem, but a people problem instead.

在 HN 中查看
MrFurious 2026/04/02 05:17
我很难相信习惯了 WordPress 的人们，尽管它有缺点也有优点（是的，wordpress 有优点），会转向这个，无论它有多少来自 Cloudflare。
I find it hard to believe that people used to WordPress, with its flaws and virtues(yes, wordpress have virtues), will switch to this, no matter how much it's from Cloudflare.
在 HN 中查看
Yokohiii 2026/04/02 09:01
> 我猜这里的很多愤世嫉俗都来自于

...事实上，CF 只是抛售代币来产生一些溢价，以与最大的单一网络平台竞争，并随意添加供应商锁定。这只是嗡嗡声，是抢占宝贵市场份额的廉价尝试。

如果你把安全性作为 EmDash 的卖点，那我就很困惑了。 WP 锁定文件有 30k 行，全新的 EmDash 有 16k 行，但它的 yaml 更简洁。 JS是WP不敢与之抗衡的反安全基石。插件隔离也是假的，WP插件是不安全的，因为它们可以访问所有内容，但它们至少需要一些，主要是数据库，这是如何解决的？隔离在那里确实很糟糕。

我不是 WP 的粉丝，但 CF 甚至没有尝试去解决这个问题。

> I'm guessing that a lot of cynicism here is coming from

...the fact that CF just dumps tokens to generate some slop to compete with the single biggest web platform and casually adding a vendor lock in. It's just buzz, an inexpensive attempt to grab a valuable market share.

If you set security as a selling point for EmDash, then I am baffled. The WP lock file has 30k lines, the brand new EmDash has 16k lines, but it LESS verbose yaml. JS is the cornerstone of anti-security that WP couldn't dare to compete with. The plugin isolation is also bogus, WP plugins are insecure because they have all access to everything, but they need at least some, mostly DB, how is that even solved? Isolation does shit there.

I am not a fan of WP, but CF doesn't even try to get this right.

thedevilslawyer 2026/04/02 12:44

Frankly, you're wrong. This is a fundamentally better plugin architecture that WP, and if you can't see it, then it's your understanding of security that's not right.

notahacker 2026/04/02 04:53
再加上吹嘘关于 slop 和 cleanroom 克隆以避免 AGPL、名称和 4 月 1 日的发布日期，如果有人关注的话，也许还会优先考虑对代理友好的加密支付基础设施。也许他们向营销代理提出“你怎么能让 HN 讨厌像开源无头 CMS 这样无害的产品？”

除此之外，如果您想要的 WordPress 部分是它的界面，而不是插件和开发人员的数量，并且不与 Cloudflare 的基础设施绑定，那么它似乎可能是一个半像样的无头 CMS。

Throw in the the bragging about slop and cleanroom clones to avoid AGPL, the name and April 1st launch date, and maybe the high priority afforded to agent-friendly crypto payment infrastructure if anyone was paying attention. Maybe they prompted the marketing agent with "how can you get HN to loathe a product as innocuous as an open source headless CMS?"

Other than that, it seems it might be a half decent headless CMS, if the bit of WordPress you want is its interface, and not the number of plugins and devs and not being tied to Cloudflare's infrastructure.

rgbrenner 2026/04/02 01:31
> 解决 WordPress 托管平台的归零问题 > WordPress 不是无服务器的

只是不准确。 WordPress 并不能阻止这种情况。托管提供商需要在其基础设施上工作，以便它可以以无服务器方式运行。

例如： https://www.agiler.io

这是无服务器 WordPress，可扩展至零。WordPress、插件或其他任何东西都没有变化。只是平台基础设施。

> Solving scale-to-zero for WordPress hosting platforms > WordPress is not serverless

Just not accurate. WordPress doesn't prevent this.. It's up to hosting providers to work on their infra so it can run in a serverless fashion.

For example: https://www.agiler.io

That's serverless wordpress that scales to zero.. no changes to WordPress, plugins or anything else.. just platform infra.

在 HN 中查看
earthlingdavey 2026/04/02 06:30
我知道你可以，但是类似博客的网站真的是岛屿架构吗？

在我看来，大多数类似的网站最好选择无框架、普通或 jQuery 来实现交互。

我无法想象普通 WP 用户会乐意迁移到 EmDash，只是为了 Astro 获得源源不断的 dependentabot 更新。

它有 55 个直接（非开发）依赖项 https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro?activeTab=dependencies - 而 ejs 有 0，nunjucks 有 3。

我厌倦了更新，也许只是我，但我对此表示怀疑。

I know you can, but do blog-like sites really Island architecture?

IMO most sites like that would be better to pick no-framework, vanilla or jQuery for interactivity.

I can't image average WP users would be happy to move to EmDash, only to have a constant stream of dependabot updates for Astro.

It has 55 direct (non-dev) dependencies https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro?activeTab=dependencies - while ejs has 0 and nunjucks has 3.

I'm weary of updates, maybe it's just me, but I doubt it.

benatkin 2026/04/02 04:03
WordPress 的一个大问题是 GPL。对此还没有太多明确的说明，我从 Automattic 听到的关于哪些代码受 GPL 覆盖、哪些代码不受 GPL 的解释来自 Automattic，而不是来自 GPL。 https://redsweater.com/blog/825/getting-pretty-lonely
A big issue with WordPress is the GPL. There hasn't been much clarity about it and the interpretations I've heard from Automattic in regard to which code is and is not covered by the GPL come from Automattic, not from the GPL. https://redsweater.com/blog/825/getting-pretty-lonely

rafark 2026/04/02 02:32

momojo 2026/04/02 08:17

在 HN 中查看
spankalee 2026/04/02 01:01
遗憾的是他们似乎没有尝试解决 CMS 和静态网站之间的分歧。

大多数 WordPress 网站可能只是静态的，但 WordPress 有一个漂亮的编辑器界面，所以它们不是静态的 - 除非您使用 SSG 插件。将其构建到核心工作流程（我相信 Astro 支持）并为用户提供一个生成静态网站的良好托管编辑器将是受欢迎的创新。

It's a shame they don't seem to try to address the divide between CMS's and static sites.

Most WordPress sites could just be static, but WordPress has a nice editor interface, so they're not - unless you use a SSG plugin. Building that into the core workflow (which I believe Astro supports) and giving users a nice hosted editor that produces a static site would be welcome innovation.

在 HN 中查看
pwython 2026/04/02 01:55
我最近将一些 WordPress 网站从 Wordpress 迁移到 Astro + Strapi，在“混合模式”下工作，因此整个网站都是静态的，除了 Strapi 中的帖子预览（只有一条路线是 SSR）。

在 Strapi 中编辑内容，一旦使用 CKEditor 等进行定制，对于熟悉 WP 的人类编辑来说，Wordpress 就足够了。

到目前为止我很喜欢堆栈。

I've been migrating a few Wordpress sites from Wordpress to Astro + Strapi recently, working in 'hybrid mode' so the entire site is static except for post previews in Strapi (only that one route is SSR).

Editing content in Strapi, once customized with CKEditor and such, is Wordpressy enough for the human Editors familiar with WP.

So far I'm loving the stack.

在 HN 中查看
MattieTK 2026/04/02 01:04
EmDash 具有一些积极的缓存和 SWR，实际上就是这样，而且我们每天都在接近这一点。当维护 CMS 数据部分的成本实际上是免费的时，您基本上是在使用静态站点。
EmDash with some aggressive caching and SWR is effectively this, and we're getting closer to that every day. When the cost of maintaining the data part of the CMS is effectively free, you're basically working with a static site anyway.

Y-bar 2026/04/02 01:32
我已经好几年没有使用 WordPress 了。但对于 WP Super Cache (1)，我们也总是这样做：在保存帖子/页面时，静态 HTML 将被写入缓存目录，并成为向访问者提供的默认内容。

[1] https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-super-cache/

I haven’t used Wordpress for a few years. But with WP Super Cache (1) we also always did pretty much that: On saving a post/page the static HTML would be written to a cache directory and be the default content served to visitors.

[1] https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-super-cache/

您只需将注释放入 firebase/supabase 等内容中，或者使用许多现成的解决方案之一。免费套餐就好了。

They can - it’s just more complex.

You just put the comments into something like firebase/supabase etc or use one of many off the shelf solutions. Free tier is fine.

在 HN 中查看
RobotToaster 2026/04/02 02:39
那么它还是静态站点吗？

您可以使用 CGI 脚本来完成此操作，无需外部依赖项，但这也不是真正静态的。

Is it still a static site then?

You could just do it with CGI scripts, without the external dependencies, but that isn't really static either.

在 HN 中查看
Closi 2026/04/02 04:05
我猜这取决于你如何称呼该架构！

我通过用 Markdown 编写帖子来运行我的本地剧院网站，然后进行一些 github 操作，使用 Hugo 将其转换为静态站点，然后将内容上传到 S3 存储桶。该网站本身具有动态内容，例如从 eventbrite 购买网站内门票以及使用外部服务发送电子邮件的联系表单。它还调用了诸如谷歌分析之类的东西。

这仍然算作静态吗？我个人认为是这样，尽管存在“动态”元素。

IMO 静态更多地指的是如何提供内容，而不是说内容不能是“动态”的，因为许多 WordPress 网站都有静态/非交互式内容，但仍然在每个页面加载时重新生成 html。

Depends what you would call that architecture then I guess!

I run my local theatre website by writing the posts in markdown, and then have some github actions which use Hugo to turn it in to a static site and then uploads the content to an S3 bucket. The site itself has dynamic content like within-website ticket buying from eventbrite and a contact form that sends email using an external service. It also calls in things like google analytics.

Does this still count as static? Personally I think so, Even though there are 'dynamic' elements.

IMO static refers more about how the content is served rather than saying that the content can’t be ‘dynamic’ as lots of Wordpress sites have static/non interactive content but still regenerate the html on each page load.

在 HN 中查看
DANmode 2026/04/02 03:20
我为我的客户运行带有嵌入式表单的静态网站。

性能表明它们肯定仍然是静态站点！

I run static sites for my clients, with embedded forms.

Performance says they’re definitely still static sites!
